pattern: Strip out every factor of two, three, and five; a positive number is ugly exactly when nothing but one remains.
hints: Only the primes 2, 3, and 5 are allowed, so divide them out and see what is left.
Non-positive numbers are never ugly; for positive ones, repeatedly remove factors of 2, then 3, then 5.
After dividing out all 2s, 3s, and 5s, the number is ugly iff the remaining value equals 1.

edge_inputs.description: zero is not positive, not ugly

stress.description: construct genuinely ugly numbers as products of 2, 3, 5 within 32-bit range
stress.note: answer always true; exercises deep factor stripping

stress.description: arbitrary integers, often carrying a forbidden prime factor
stress.note: covers negatives, zero, and non-ugly positives
